1.21 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:

regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or
less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to
the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the
scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full
implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for
in COA Circular 2020-006.
2. Require the MA and PC- Designate to ensure the immediate preparation
of the PLCs and PCs after the implementation of “One-time cleansing of
PPE accounts” and to conduct regularly the annual reconciliation of
their records.

sending the employee to trainings and seminars on Property and Supply
Management and obtaining updated COA Circulars and manuals.

2.19 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
1. The MHO personnel concerned to submit immediately to the Audit
Team, through the Accountant, the following documents to establish
their claim to SRAs under AO Nos. 36 and 42:
a. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance/office
order/authority to report for work;
b. Daily Time Records with Biometric printouts/copies of logbook or
Certificate of Attendance in the COVID Facility/Center by their
immediate supervisor during the period of claims;
c. Accomplishment reports indicating the number of COVID patients
attended to in a day; and
d. For health workers not directly catering COVID-19 patients, an
explanation as to the scientific basis for the premise that healthcare
workers not directly catering to COVID-19 patients are equally
exposed to COVID-19 and can be considered “in contact with
COVID-19 patients,” and thus should be given the SRA.
2. The MA to reclassify the payments of SRA amounting to
₱248,909.19from Hazard Pay to Other Personnel Benefits (regular
employees) and Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses
(contractual employees) in the CY 2021 Statement of Financial
Performance.

COA; and

provisions stated in the GPPB Circular 04-2020.

System.

We recommended that  the  LCE  requires  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
prepare SLs to support the  balances  of  Due  to  National  Government
Agencies and Due to Local Government Units  accounts  for  the  ensuing
year, and submit the same to the Audit Team for review purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  requires  the  Municipal
Accountant to:

a. evaluate the discrepancies between the balances of the  loans  per
   books and per records of the banks that  granted  the  loans,  and
   prepare  the  necessary   adjusting   entries   to   correct   the
   deficiencies;

b. prepare SLs to support the balance of  Loans  Payable  –  Domestic
   account for the ensuing year and submit the same to the Audit Team
   for review purposes;

c. properly classify the current  portion  of  the  Loans  Payable  –
   Domestic at year-end in compliance with IPSAS No. 1; and

d. present  sufficient  and  appropriate  disclosures  on  the  Loans
   Payable – Domestic in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide
   adequate information on the financial status of the LGU.

We recommended that  the  LCE  enjoins  the  Municipal  Accountant  and
Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 52 of the General Provisions
of the GAA of FY 2019 in the computation and disbursement  of  salaries
and wages of agency officials and employees.

We recommended that the LCE be more judicious in  authorizing  employees
to attend seminars/conventions/assemblies.  In  choosing  whom  to  send
to a particular training, the LCE should  consider   the   employee’s
position/function, qualifications,  and  ability  to  impart  knowledge
acquired from the training, among others.

This recommendation is without prejudice to  the  issuance  of  Notice/s
of Disallowance if the agency fails to justify the  necessity  of  sending
the employees concerned to attend the seminars/conventions/assemblies.

We recommended that the LCE requires the Municipal Accountant  and  the
Municipal Treasurer to withhold the correct amount of  taxes  from  all
honoraria payments, and remit  the  same  to  the  Bureau  of  Internal
Revenue within the reglementary period, pursuant to Sections 5 and 7 of
RR No. 11-2018.

We recommended that the LCE: a. require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain and update PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each class of PPE, and reconcile their records regularly; b. cause the conduct of physical count of properties annually, and the preparation of the RPCPPE, and submit the same to COA within the reglementary period; and c. require the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation for all infrastructure assets, and to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and all others concerned to desist from including lump-sum appropriations in the 20% DF and from using the said fund for disbursements which are not allowed to be charged therefrom. Henceforth, utilize properly the 20% Development Fund, pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and RA No. 7160. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice/s of Suspension, where necessary.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities. Moreover, the LCE should require: a) the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries (RAOs); b) the LDRRMO to prepare monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the same within the reglementary period; c) the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records for the LDRRMF and provide adequate disclosures thereof in the Notes to Financial Statements; and d) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended CY 2018 LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund; pursuant to NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC (JMC) No. 2014-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to enforce settlement of the liquidated damages by the contractor/suppliers concerned. Henceforth, strictly monitor and enforce compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects, to ensure that they are delivered completely and implemented satisfactorily, and on time; and see to it that the corresponding liquidated damages are deducted from the claims of the contractors/suppliers concerned, where appropriate.

We recommended that LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to strictly enforce compliance with Section 6 of Annex E of the RIRR of RA 9184 in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the withholding of 10% retention money from the progress payments to contractors.

We recommended that the LCE oblige the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to correctly withhold the taxes on all payments to contractors/suppliers who are subject to the 5% and 3% VAT, and 1% EWT for goods and 2% EWT for services, and remit the same to the BIR on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to reconcile the reciprocal accounts in the books for all funds and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in the financial statements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and the PPSAS.

We recommended that management consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the MOA to include provisions on: The period within which to submit the liquidation reports by the concerned barangays; The delay in the submission of liquidation reports of the transferred funds should disqualify the delinquent barangay/s from receiving additional funds for its future projects. We also recommended that the Mayor should require all Barangays requesting for financial assistance from the Municipal Government to submit the necessary plans and specifications of the project, the map indicating where these are to be undertaken, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. If possible seek the help of private companies/organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste and “no littering” policies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the implementation of the LGSF-ADM projects be within its targeted timeline and strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-03. Furthermore, we recommended that the Mayor require the Accountant to submit copies of all documents to support the status of the project implementation.

We recommended that management refrain from using the 20% Development Fund for expenditures that do not qualify as investment and capital expenditures. Henceforth, strictly observe the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 on the appropriation and utilization of the development fund. We also recommended that management should submit regularly and on time the utilization of the fund for the current and continuing appropriations with its supporting documents.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be required to transfer to the Special Trust Fund the unexpended balances of LDRRMF for CY 2017 and the prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all claims are adequately supported with complete and proper documents as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001. Likewise, prepare and submit the Oblication Slips for the disbursement vouchers submitted together with the approved SEF Budget. We also recommended that the Local School Board and other concerned Municipal Officials strictly implement the provisions of Sections 4.0 and 5.0 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipality comply with the provision of establishing the Petty Cash Fund as follows: Require the Petty Cash Custodian to use the petty cash fund only for the recurring petty operating expenses of the LGU. Discontinue the practice of procuring office supplies, materials and spare parts through reimbursement per office. Instead all procurements should be included in the Annual Procurement Plan and to be procured in bulk in the Procurements Services for the common-used supplies and through the provision on RA 9184 for other supplies and materials. Complete information and specific details should be indicated in the Petty Cash Voucher especially those claims for meals and snacks and discontinue the practice of charging to the petty cash fund the meals and snacks during travel. Ensure that all charges to the Petty Cash Fund are supported with complete documentation as prescribed in Item 1.2.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be directed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents, and to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts as provided under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 39(1) and 39(3) of PD 1445.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to keep and maintain a check register where all checks drawn for the day, including cancelled ones, are immediately recorded in chronological sequence and where the rightful claimants are required to acknowledge receipt of the check thereon. Further, cancelled checks should be submitted for audit to prove their actual status.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to correct the error in the recording of the fund transfer from the General Fund for the BUB checks issued, the LGU counterpart fund for BUB projects, and the receipt of funds from the National Government Agencies.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly observe the prohibition on splitting of contracts in its procurement transactions. We further recommend for the immediate submission of the lacking documents noted in the sampled transactions. Henceforth, adequate and proper documentation of all bidding procedures prescribed in the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001 shall be complied with.

We recommended that the BAC strictly observe the procurement processes as outlined in the IRR of RA 9184 and its relevant Annexes/Appendixes. Furthermore, ensure the submission of the supporting procurement documents.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and its support groups strictly adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA 9184 and its IRR, including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the conduct of a strict evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the submission of complete contract documents.

We recommended that management implement the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003, involve closely the Barangay officials and civic leaders in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws, and intensify the information campaign at the household level on the ordinances issued by the Municipality. If possible seek the help of private companies/organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste and “no littering” policies.

The Municipal Accountant has assured the audit team that he will
immediately make adjustments in their books of account that the audit team could

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant closely monitor the LGU’s

be imposed against responsible officials.

a) The period within which to submit the liquidation reports by the

b) The delay in the submission of liquidation reports of the transferred

Development fund for expenditures that do not qualify as investment and capital

required to transfer to the Special Trust Fund the unexpended balances of

Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and its required breakdown, and provide
adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover, require the
LDRRMO to submit justification on the variances between the summary and
breakdown reports. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002
and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.
Management commented that all findings and recommendations pertaining to
the LDRRMF are noted. The audit team was also informed through their written reply
that the amount of P1,576,715.34 was already transferred to the LDRRMF account on

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all claims
are adequately supported with complete and proper documents as required
under COA Circular No. 2012-001. Likewise, prepare and submit the
Obligation Slips for the disbursement vouchers submitted together with the
approved SEF Budget. We also recommend that the Local School Board and
other concerned Municipal Officials strictly implement the provisions of
Sections 4.0 and 5.0 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
In their written reply, management promised to comply strictly with the
required documentations for the year 2018.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other concerned
Municipal Officials to allocate 1% of the IRA for the strengthening and
implementation of the programs, projects and activities of Local Council for the
Protection of Children and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of the
Republic Act 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
Management commented that the Budget Officer failed to attach the list of
programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the Local Council for the Protection of
Children (LCPC) in the compiled 2017 Annual Budget that was submitted to the
Sanggunian Panlalawigan since it was not a compulsory attachment to the annual
budget compilation. Moreover, the list of PPAs for the LCPC was already
incorporated in the CY 2018 Annual Budget.

We recommended that the Municipality comply with the provisions of
RA 9184 and its IRR in the hiring of consultants:
a) The BAC shall observe the procedures required in Item 7 of Annex H of the
2016 revised IRR of RA 9184.
b) The BAC shall undertake negotiations with, and validate the capability of the
individual consultants to undertake and fulfill the consultancy work based on
the Terms of Reference, and to recommend to the Mayor the award of the
contract.
c) In accordance with Section 54.6 (e) of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184,
consultants are mandated to register with PhilGEPS and provide a
PhilGEPS Registration Number as a condition for the award of the contract.

We recommended that management procure telephone/communications
services for cellular/mobile phones in accordance with Government
Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184) and its IRR. Likewise, officials and
employees who are entitled to the Telephone Expenses allocation/budget should
be in line with item No. 5, Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-003.
The Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to
require that all claims for telephone/communication services under MOOE be
supported with Statement of Account/Bill from telecommunication service
providers, official receipts/invoices and certification that calls are official as
required in COA Circular No. 2012-001. Likewise, official receipts/invoices
printed in thermal papers should be photocopied.
Moreover, the monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a
rate not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and beyond
just measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into
account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various
telecommunication service providers.
In addition, we recommended that the municipality submit a copy of existing
memorandum or order and Sanggunian Bayan Resolution detailing the
guidelines for all claims of the Telephone Expense and the corresponding rates
entitled for each official or employee.
In their written reply, management was amenable of the suggestions and
recommendations. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor assured the audit team that he will
immediately make an executive order on the limit on the use of the communication
expenses. Moreover, they will verify all pertinent transactions if the statement of
accounts and other supporting documents are attached and they will avoid claims on
reimbursement basis. And with respect to the three employees lower than Division
Chief who received/claimed telephone expenses, they were functioning as head of
office during those periods when they submitted claims for telephone expenses.

b. Discontinue the practice of procuring office supplies, materials and
spare parts through reimbursement per office. Instead all
procurements should be included in the Annual Procurement Plan
and to be procured in bulk in the Procurements Services for the
common-used supplies and through the provision on RA 9184 for
other supplies and materials.

Petty Cash Voucher especially those claims for meals and snacks and
discontinue the practice of charging to the petty cash fund the meals
and snacks during travel.
d. Ensure that all charges to the Petty Cash Fund are supported with
complete documentation as prescribed in Item 1.2.2 of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

service for the entire LGU when they had those transactions charge to petty cash fund
and through the AOM they are thankful that they were reminded to strictly adhere to
RA 9184. Moreover, for their CY 2018 transactions management will adhere to the
provisions on the use of the Petty Cash Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be
required to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts and financial
reports in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, Section 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of
COA Circular No. 96-001 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer be directed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers
together with their supporting documents, and to strictly observe the timely
submission of accounts as provided under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 39(1) and 39(3) of PD 1445.
Management commented that they will immediately submit the original copies
of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents.

We recommended that management formulate an action plan to
implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly
accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the
House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as
mandated by Section 96 of RA 10717.
The AAPSI for CY 2016 was submitted by the Municipal Accountant only on
April 23, 2018.

keep and maintain a check register where all checks drawn for the day,

and where the rightful claimants are required to acknowledge receipt of the
check thereon. Further, cancelled checks should be submitted for audit to prove
their actual status.

cancelled are all accounted for and there has been no loss of government funds. She
admitted that there had been inadvertent lapses on her part and that of her staff on the
issuance of checks in chronological order. She has promised to maintain check
register in the issuing year.

observations.reason: Programs, projects and activities were already more than 5% of total budget. See related discussion in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management consider the improvement of the existing clauses in the MOA to include provisions on: a) The period within which to submit the liquidation reports by the concerned LGUs; b) The delay in the submission of liquidation reports of the transferred funds should disqualify the delinquent barangay/s from receiving additional funds for its future projects. We further recommend that management review its provisions in the MOA if these are doable and not in violation of other rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Municipal Finding No. 9 instructed the concerned/ officials to comply.
observations.reason: See related discussion in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the overstatement of the expense account and understatement of 'Due from LGU account' by P700,000.00. Likewise, he should maintain subsidiary records to properly account for the release and utilization of the fund transfer to other LGUs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Municipal Finding No. 9
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has assured the audit team that he will immediately make adjustments in their books of account that the audit team could vouched upon submission of their accounts in April, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Local School Board to prepare an Annual School Board Budget to be approved by all members of the Local School Board as required in Section 99 of RA 7160 and duly supported with programs, projects and activities that are aligned with Section 100(c) of RA 7160, and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, series of 1998 and 01-B, series of 2001. Likewise, strictly observe the limitations on the use of the SEF. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the misstatements on some accounts due to bookkeeping errors.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Municipal Finding No. 10
observations.reason: AAR 2015 related findings; management submitted documents and actions were taken but some issues remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be required to transfer to the Special Trust Fund the unexpended balances of LDRRMF for CY 2015 and 2016. Likewise, require the LDRRMO, Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to maintain separate registries and subsidiary records for the LDRRMF and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Municipal Finding No. 11 instructed the concerned/ officials to comply.
observations.reason: The Partially implemented adjustments were made. Management commented that the amount of P1,576,715.34 was already transferred to the LDRRMF account on February 18, 2018 and they will closely coordinate with the audit team as to the correct amount that must be deposited to the LDRRMF account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implement the five environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment and involve closely the Barangay officials and civic leaders in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws, and intensify the information campaign at the household level on the ordinances issued by the Municipality. If possible seek the help of private companies/organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste and 'no littering' policies.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Municipal Finding No. 12
observations.reason: Solid Waste segregation in the Barangay Level is not yet fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 96 of RA 10717.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Municipal Finding No. 13
observations.reason: The AAPSI for CY 2016 was submitted by the Municipal Accountant only on April 23, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant be required to maintain and update property cards per category of assets, and reconcile their records regularly. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the drugs and medicines inventory account which have been dormant for years.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Finding No. 1 AAR 2013 to the new property custodian.
observations.reason: The new property custodian has not yet conducted the physical count of PPEs. The Municipal Accountant has not yet conducted the full analysis; IIRUP and other reports pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 14 of Executive Order No. 298 and COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Likewise, require the Accountant to maintain subsidiary records of unliquidated cash advances and to exert extra efforts in identifying the particulars/information of the unliquidated cash advances to determine the persons responsible for the liquidation of the amount.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Finding No. 2
observations.reason: The LGU has substantially reduced the unliquidated cash advances. There are still unliquidated cash advances & pre-payments. The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the Journal Entry Voucher to effect the adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher to reclassify the fund transfers inadvertently debited to account 'Subsidy to Other Funds', to the appropriate asset account 'Due from GOCC' pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Finding No. 3
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the Journal Entry Voucher to show the reclassification of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer return/refund the amount of P36,000.00 to DSWD Field Office VIII. Henceforth, the DSWD Field Office should fully comply with the DSWD Department Order No. 15, series of 2010 and the memorandum of agreement it entered with the LGU relative to the implementation of the Social Pension Program.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Finding No. 4
observations.reason: The concerned official had refunded the unclaimed pension to DSWD Field Office VIII. DSWD Field Office VIII responded accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management see to it that at least 5% of the total budget is allocated to the GAD programs. Likewise, establish a GAD Focal Point System and ensure its functionality. Moreover, prepare the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Reports and submit the same to DILG. Henceforth, see to it that the details or breakdown is provided for the utilization report to ensure that the expenditures are aligned with the GAD Plan.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Finding No. 5
observations.reason: GAD code was initiated and GAD plan and budget were prepared and submitted to DILG. GAD expenditures were aligned and more than 5% of budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant be required to submit the accounts and strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-001 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 39 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Finding No. 9 called the attention of the concerned offices and/or Municipal Officials that reports and related accounts must be submitted on time.
observations.reason: There were still delays in the submission of accounts in some offices and/or some persons responsible.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be directed to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents, and to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts as provided under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Sections 39(1) and 39(3) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Finding No. 10
observations.reason: Management submitted the paid disbursement vouchers which they failed to submit during audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the municipal treasurer to make proper markings on the voucher and supporting documents to indicate that the same has been paid to avoid re-use.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Municipal Finding No. 11
observations.reason: All paid disbursement vouchers are now stamped "PAID".

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly require the concerned officials to submit copies of contracts, purchase orders and job order contracts within the prescribed period in adherence to the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, and to ensure that contracts are funded by proper authorities before these are perfected or executed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Municipal Finding No. 12 reiterated the requirement.
observations.reason: There were still delays in submission of documents. Some were submitted later or during meetings/memos.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to reclassify unserviceable properties to 'Other Assets' account from the Furniture and Fixtures account and prepare the required Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) which shall be used as basis to drop from the books the unserviceable properties carried in the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts. Moreover, require the Disposal Committee to initiate an action for the final disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Municipal Finding No. 1 instructed the Municipal Property custodian to record or update the IIRUP and the Municipal Accountant to update the proper classification of PPEs.
observations.reason: IIRUP and reports were not prepared for all items. Some inventory and disposals pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to reclassify purchases of various equipment amounting to P177,327.30 to IT Equipment and IT Software account and Furniture and Fixtures account as appropriate and draw the Journal Entry Voucher to effect the reclassification.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Municipal Finding No. 2 adjustments have already been made.
observations.reason: Adjustments were made but the Municipal Accountant failed to submit the Journal Entry Voucher to show the adjustments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal entry voucher to correct the overstatement of the 'Due to Other NGAs' account and maintain subsidiary records to properly account for the receipt and utilization of the trust fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Municipal Finding No. 5 adjusted the said accounts. Likewise, he should maintain subsidiary records to properly account for the receipt and utilization of the trust fund.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the said accounts. He should maintain subsidiary records for the receipt and utilization of the trust fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor directs the Revenue Collection Clerks to turnover their collections daily to the Municipal Treasurer and for the latter to remit the daily collections to the depository bank; and to ensure that the collections are deposited daily and intact to prevent accumulation and exposure of funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Municipal Finding No. 8 minimized the delays in some Collection Clerks daily remittance. The Mayor called the attention of concerned offices and Municipal Officials that reports and related accounts must be submitted on time.
observations.reason: There are still delays in the turnover of collections of some Collection Clerks and remittances to the depository were not made daily and intact in some instances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide the basic information in the Purchase Order, Request for Quotations and Purchase Request as required under COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 and RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Municipal Finding No. 9 directed the concerned LGU officials to comply with COA Circular.
observations.reason: Some specifications and basic data were not included in some PRs/POs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and look into the causes of the variances and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with correct account balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Municipal Finding No. 6 the Municipal Accountant already made journal entries to adjust the accounts.
observations.reason: There are still variances between the books and actual remittances in some months; some adjustments were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the guidelines set forth in RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations in all their procurement activities. We also recommend that management adopt a policy on the grant of reimbursements or allowance to officers and employees for uniformity in application and for guidance in succeeding transactions. Moreover, strictly comply with documentary requirements for all types of transactions as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Municipal Finding No. 11 advised management to minimize reimbursements and petty cash disbursements; an instruction was given to comply with procurement rules and proper documentation.
observations.reason: Some transactions were still made on reimbursement basis and through petty cash fund; management to comply with procurement requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the grant of Intelligence Fund to the Advances to Officers and Employees account to come up with correct account balances. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor should immediately submit his liquidation reports on the cash advances for intelligence fund directly to the COA Chairman’s Office pursuant to COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Municipal Finding No. 11 instructed the municipal accountant to reclassify the grant to Advances to Officers and Employees account.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant recorded the reclassification having no evidence at hand; some transactions still pending proper supporting documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry
voucher to correct the error in the recording of the fund transfer from the
General Fund for the BUB checks issued, the LGU counterpart fund for BUB
projects, and the receipt of funds from the National Government Agencies.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry
voucher to record the donated equipment to its proper asset accounts and
provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover,
require the Property and Supply unit to record the other in-kind donations in
the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM. Henceforth, adhere strictly to
COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of
the LDRRMF.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly
observe the prohibition on splitting of contracts in its procurement transactions.
We further recommend for the immediate submission of the lacking documents
noted in the sampled transactions.Henceforth, adequate and proper
documentation of all bidding procedures prescribed in the Implementing
Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001 shall be
complied with.

We recommend that the BAC strictly observe the procurement processes
as outlined in the IRR of RA 9184 and its relevant Annexes/Appendixes.Submit
the lacking procurement documents and review the data on the RFQ and
Contracts entered into by the LGU.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee and its support
groups strictly adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA 9184 and its
IRR, including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the
conduct of a strict evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the
submission of complete contract documents.

We recommend that management discontinue the practice of procuring
consulting services without undertaking the mandatory eligibility checks and
short-listing processes, pursuant to the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommend that management refrain from using the 20%
Development fund to pay expenditures that do not partakethe nature of
investment and capital expenditures. Henceforth, strictly observe the provisions
of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 on
the appropriation and utilization of the development fund.
We further recommend that the lump-sum appropriations shall be
supported by sufficient details showing the priority list of projects in the selected
barangays and the applicable amounts.
A sound internal control policy, such as regular preparation and
submission of the 20% DF utilization report and the creation of a working group
to monitor implementation of the projects in constituent barangays, may
likewise need to be developed in order to ensure that the fund was utilized in
accordance with its purpose.

We recommend that management consider the improvement of the
existing clauses in the MOA to include provisions on:
a) The period to submit the liquidation reports by the concerned
recipient- LGUs.
b) The non-implementation or non-liquidation of the transferred funds
within the allotted timeframe would disqualify the delinquent
barangay/s from being a recipient of LGU-Motiong funds for its
future projects.
We further recommend that management review its provisions in the
MOA if these are doable and not in violations of other rules and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a
journal entry voucher to correct the overstatement of the expense account and
understatement of “Due from LGU account”. Likewise, he should maintain
subsidiary records to properly account for the release and utilization of the fund
transfer to other LGUs.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board prepare an Annual
School Board Budget to be approved by all members of the Local School Board
as required in Section 99 of RA 7160 and duly supported with programs,
projects and activities that are aligned with Section 100 (c) of RA 7160, and
DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, series of 1998 and 01-B, series of
2001. Likewise, strictly observe the limitations on the use of the Special
Education Fund (SEF). Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to draw a
journal entry voucher to correct the misstatements on some accounts due to
bookkeeping errors.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant
berequired to transfer to the Special Trust Fund the unexpended balances of
LDRRMF for CY 2015 and 2016. Likewise, require the Municipal Budget
Officer and Accountant to maintain separate registries and subsidiary records
for the LDRRMF and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and
2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.

We recommend that management implement the five basic
environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required
under R.A. No. 9003, involve closely the Barangay officials and civic leaders in
the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws, and intensify the
information campaign at the household level on the ordinances issued by the
Municipality. If possible seek the help of private companies/organizations in
the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the
proper segregation of waste and “no littering” policies.

We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement
the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished
AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee
on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by
Section 93 of RA 10651.
